verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient has been diagnosed with breast cancer.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This requires synthesizing information from both the patient chart and the script. In the chart Onc Patient 9, tab clinical notes, row 203, column E, it states, "Malignant neoplasm of upper-outer quadrant of right breast in female." The script indicates, "You were diagnosed several years ago with cancer. You were cancer-free for a while, but the same cancer you had before has returned." This implies the patient has been diagnosed with breast cancer.

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient's breast cancer is metastatic.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This is indicated by information in the script. "About 18 months ago, scans showed that the cancer had returned in the bones and a few small spots in the liver." Because the cancer is spreading to the liver and bones, it is metastatic.

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient has already had endocrine therapy.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
"You started an aromatase inhibitor along with a CDK4/6 inhibitor, which controlled the disease for more than a year. When the last scan showed growth in the liver and new bone lesions, we switched you to fulvestrant injections." This is from the script. Fulvestrant and aromatase inhibitor therapy both qualify as endocrine therapy. "Aromatase inhibitors are a group of endocrine agents." https://www.bcm.edu/healthcare/specialties/oncology/cancer-treatment/chemotherapy/endocrine-therapy. 

"Fulvestrant is a novel endocrine therapy for breast cancer." https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/20156170/

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient's cancer has an ESR1 mutation.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This is clearly stated in the attached lab results. "ESR1	Positive."

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient's cancer is HER2 negative.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This is clearly stated in the attached lab results. "HER2	Negative."

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient does not want elacestrant.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This is from the attached script. Patient: “Yes, please not elacestrant.”

verifier_values.criteria: States that the most appropriate next treatment option is imlunestrant.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: The next, best treatment option is imlunestrant. "Imlunestrant is indicated for the treatment of adults with ER-positive, HER2-negative, ESR1-mutated advanced or metastatic breast cancer with disease progression following at least one line of endocrine therapy." The patient matches all indications for this treatment option. https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2023/217639s000lbl.pdf

verifier_values.criteria: States that imlunestrant is dosed orally.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This is the route of administration of imlunestrant. "The recommended dosage is 400 mg orally once daily" https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2025/218881s000lbl.pdf

verifier_values.criteria: States that imlunestrant is taken once daily.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This is the frequency of imlunestrant. "The recommended dosage is 400 mg orally once daily" https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2025/218881s000lbl.pdf

verifier_values.criteria: States that imlunestrant is dosed at 400 mg.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This is the dosing of imlunestrant. "The recommended dosage is 400 mg orally once daily" https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2025/218881s000lbl.pdf
